document.title: Security & Permission Extension Model — LAWIM_V2
document.description: Complete structured extension model for LAWIM_V2 Security & Permission domain. Covers 4-level permission model, matrix, scope, approval workflow, consent, audit logging, temporary grants, org hierarchy, consent derivation, and relationship-based permissions.

permission_levels.business_reason: Formal approval workflow for agency creation, listing validation, professional verification. Highest permission tier enabling authoritative actions on platform resources.
permission_levels.insufficient_current_model: No explicit approval level or workflow exists in V2. Approve actions are either hardcoded admin checks or absent entirely.
permission_levels.proposed_target: Implement Approval permission level (A) as fourth tier in permission model. Actions requiring A: agency_creation, listing_validation, professional_verification, organization_validation, refund_approval, account_suspension, account_deletion, permission_grant, temporary_permission, document_validation, transaction_validation.
permission_levels.impact: Security-critical function governance; multi-person approval gates; audit trail for sensitive actions
permission_levels.decision_question: Which specific actions require Approve-level permission beyond the default list

permission_levels.business_reason: Formalize implicit read access into explicit permission level with scope awareness (own vs managed vs org vs all). Foundation for all data access control.
permission_levels.insufficient_current_model: V2 read access is implicit per role with no explicit permission check system. No scope differentiation between own/org/all resources.
permission_levels.proposed_target: Implement explicit Read permission check system. Apply scope resolution: own (self-created/owned), managed (assigned/team), org (organization), all (system-wide). Read access defaults to 'own' unless matrix specifies wider scope.
permission_levels.impact: Access control granularity; data confidentiality; foundation for all higher permission levels
permission_levels.decision_question: Default read scope for partner and user roles across all resource types

permission_levels.business_reason: Formalize per-role creation rights with scope awareness. Determines which resources each role can instantiate.
permission_levels.insufficient_current_model: V2 creation rights are role-dependent but hardcoded per feature with no formal matrix. Users can create projects but not organizations; operators can create listings but not services.
permission_levels.proposed_target: Formalize Create permission level per role in permission matrix. Creation scope limited by role: admin (all), manager (org-scoped), operator (org-scoped), partner (own-scoped), user (own personal resources only).
permission_levels.impact: Feature-level access control; permission consistency across all resource types

permission_levels.business_reason: Formalize edit scope with three tiers: own (self-created), managed (items-under-responsibility), all (any resource). Critical for data integrity.
permission_levels.insufficient_current_model: V2 edit access granted by ownership or organization membership with no formal items-under-responsibility concept. Agents cannot be explicitly assigned management scope.
permission_levels.proposed_target: Formalize Edit permission scope with three tiers: own (self resources), managed (assigned/team resources), all (any resource). Add managed scope via assignment tracking (property agent_id, lead routed_to_agent_id, project assigned_agent_id).
permission_levels.impact: Access control granularity; data integrity; enables agent/property management workflows

permission_matrix.concept: Permission matrix (Role × Resource) with all resources
permission_matrix.business_reason: Complete definition of which roles can perform which actions on which resources. Single source of truth for all authorization decisions. Covers 22 resource types across 5 roles.
permission_matrix.insufficient_current_model: V2 has no formal permission matrix. Authorization is implemented ad-hoc per endpoint with role checks.
permission_matrix.proposed_target: Implement complete permission matrix as configuration-driven lookup table. Matrix covers: Property, Project, Transaction, User, Organization, OrganizationMember, Service, ServiceOrder, Payment, Lead, LeadPurchase, Document, Visit, Match, Conversation, Negotiation, AgentCredit, CRM settings, System config, ApprovalWorkflow, Consent record, PermissionAuditLog, TemporaryPermission.
permission_matrix.impact: Unified authorization; systematic permission checking; audit-ready permission model
permission_matrix.decision_question: Permission matrix granularity: single combined matrix or separate system/org/relationship sub-matrices

crud_vsra.operator: RCE on 16/22 resources (org-scoped on Property/Project, own elsewhere)
crud_vsra.partner: RCE on 6/22 (own), R on 10/22 (org-scoped), otherwise none

permission_scope.business_reason: Scope-based permission resolution enables fine-grained access control beyond flat role checks. Same role may have different effective permissions based on resource relationship.
permission_scope.insufficient_current_model: V2 has basic own/org distinction but no managed scope (items-under-responsibility) and no formal scope resolution engine.
permission_scope.proposed_target: Implement 4-tier scope resolution: own (direct FK), managed (assignment FK), org (org membership), all (system-wide). Resolution priority: explicit deny > managed > org > own > all. Scope mapping table per resource type with resolution function.
permission_scope.impact: Granular access control; enables delegation workflow; foundation for relationship-based permissions

crud_vsra.manager: org scope default, all scope on specific resources (Property(all)/Project(all)/Transaction(all))
crud_vsra.operator: org scope on Property/Project, own scope elsewhere
crud_vsra.partner: org scope on read, own scope on edit

approval_workflow.business_reason: Structured approval process for actions requiring multi-person authorization. Enables security gates for agency creation, listing validation, professional verification, refunds, and account management.
approval_workflow.insufficient_current_model: No approval workflow entity exists in V2. All approval-like actions are handled via ad-hoc admin checks or do not exist.
approval_workflow.proposed_target: Create ApprovalWorkflow entity with attributes: id, approval_type, target_type, target_id, requested_by, assigned_to, status (pending/approved/rejected/cancelled/escalated), reviewed_by, reviewed_at, approval_notes, rejection_reason, escalated_to, escalated_at, escalation_reason, approval_deadline, sla_breached, auto_approve_on_timeout, created_at. Implement 12 approval types with specific approval chains.
approval_workflow.impact: Security compliance; multi-person authorization; audit trail for sensitive actions
approval_workflow.decision_question: Approval routing rules: which approval types require which approver role and escalation path

definition.description: Approval requests are created by requesters, assigned to approvers

crud_vsra.operator: R (assigned approvals), C (create for own actions)

consent_management.concept: Consent management model with derivation rules
consent_management.business_reason: Structured consent tracking for data sharing, contact revelation, lead routing, GDPR compliance, and double consent workflow. Each consent type derives specific permissions when active.
consent_management.insufficient_current_model: No consent management model exists in V2. Double consent for contact is handled implicitly if at all. No GDPR consent tracking.
consent_management.proposed_target: Create Consent entity with attributes: id, consent_type (10 types), granted_by, granted_to, target_type, target_id, status (active/revoked/expired/superseded), scope (single_use/limited/ongoing/permanent), expires_at, revoked_at, revoked_by, consent_channel, consent_proof, terms_version, created_at. Implement derivation mapping from each consent type to specific permissions.
consent_management.impact: GDPR compliance; double consent enforcement; consent-based permission derivation; audit trail
consent_management.decision_question: Consent types required for MVP vs full set; GDPR processing consent scope

definition.description: Consent records are created by users (self-consent) or system (derived). Readable by consent parties and authorized roles.

permission_audit_logging.concept: Permission audit logging with retention policy
permission_audit_logging.business_reason: Complete audit trail for all permission changes, consent actions, approval decisions, and access denials. Security compliance and intrusion detection.
permission_audit_logging.insufficient_current_model: V2 has generic Event model but no permission-specific audit logging. No structured event types for permission changes, no retention policy, no access denial logging.
permission_audit_logging.proposed_target: Create PermissionAuditLog entity with 18 event types covering permission lifecycle. Implement mandatory logging for: permission grants/revocations/denials, role/scope changes, consent grants/revocations, temporary grant lifecycle, approval decisions, access breaches, sharing activations. Retention policy: 5 years for security events, 1 year for denials, 2 years for temporary grants.
permission_audit_logging.impact: Security compliance (GDPR, SOX); intrusion detection; audit trail; forensic capabilities
permission_audit_logging.decision_question: Permission audit retention periods by event category

definition.description: Audit logs are system-created only. Readable by authorized roles. Immutable — no modify/delete.

temporary_permissions.business_reason: Time-bound permission grants for delegation, emergency access, cross-org collaboration, auditor access, and support operations. Automatic expiry prevents permission sprawl.
temporary_permissions.insufficient_current_model: No temporary permission mechanism exists in V2. All permissions are permanent until role change or manual revocation.
temporary_permissions.proposed_target: Create TemporaryPermission entity with attributes: id, granted_by, granted_to, resource_type, resource_id, permission_level, permission_scope, reason, starts_at, expires_at, max_extensions, extension_count, status (active/expired/revoked/extended), revoked_at, revoked_by, revocation_reason, created_at. Implement 6 grant scenarios with specified durations and approval requirements. Auto-revoke via scheduled task at expires_at.
temporary_permissions.impact: Operational flexibility; security (auto-cleanup); delegation support; audit trail for all temporary grants
temporary_permissions.decision_question: Temporary grant scenarios: which delegation patterns require implementation first

definition.description: Temporary grants created by authorized granters. Automatically expire. Revocable by granters and admins.

organization_permission_hierarchy.concept: Organization permission hierarchy (responsible > admin > agent > assistant)
organization_permission_hierarchy.business_reason: 4-tier agency hierarchy with distinct permission levels within the organization scope. Enables org structure with clear responsibility boundaries.
organization_permission_hierarchy.insufficient_current_model: V2 Organization model has basic user membership but no agency-specific role hierarchy on OrganizationMember. No differentiated org-scoped permissions.
organization_permission_hierarchy.proposed_target: Implement 4-tier agency hierarchy on OrganizationMember: agency_role enum (responsible/admin/agent/assistant). Permission mapping: responsible (full org control RCEA), admin (org RCEA except org settings), agent (own RCE + assigned R), assistant (assigned R only). Intersection with V2 role for effective permission calculation.
organization_permission_hierarchy.impact: Agency management; org-scoped permission granularity; responsible accountability
organization_permission_hierarchy.decision_question: Agency hierarchy granularity: 4 tiers or simplified (responsible/agent only) for MVP

organization_permission_hierarchy.concept: Agency role intersection with V2 role for effective permission
organization_permission_hierarchy.business_reason: Effective permission is the intersection of V2 role permission and agency role permission within org scope. Prevents privilege escalation where an agency role could exceed V2 role limitations.
organization_permission_hierarchy.insufficient_current_model: No dual-role permission model exists in V2. Users have a single V2 role with no org-scoped sub-role.
organization_permission_hierarchy.proposed_target: Implement permission intersection: effective_permission = min(V2_role_permission, agency_role_permission) for org-scoped resources. For non-org resources, only V2 role applies. Resolution examples: operator+admin → org RCEA, user+agent → own RCE + org R, partner+admin → org R-only.
organization_permission_hierarchy.impact: Security boundary enforcement; prevents privilege escalation; clean role hierarchy
organization_permission_hierarchy.decision_question: Permission intersection rules: strict min() or custom mapping per role pair

crud_vsra.resolution_rule: effective = intersection(V2_role_matrix_row, agency_role_scope) for org resources; V2_role_matrix_row for non-org resources

consent_derived_permissions.concept: Permission derivation from consent with revocation cascade
consent_derived_permissions.business_reason: Specific permissions are derived from active consent grants. When consent is revoked, all derived permissions are immediately invalidated. Critical for GDPR compliance and double consent workflow.
consent_derived_permissions.insufficient_current_model: No consent-to-permission derivation exists in V2. Consent is not tracked; permissions are not conditional on consent status.
consent_derived_permissions.proposed_target: Implement consent-to-permission derivation mapping: 10 consent types → specific permissions. Rules: double consent → contact read, lead purchase → lead data access, data sharing → profile read, agent opt-in → lead receipt. Implement revocation cascade: consent revoked → derived permissions suspended → audit logged → downstream services notified.
consent_derived_permissions.impact: GDPR compliance; consent enforcement; double consent integrity; permission lifecycle management
consent_derived_permissions.decision_question: Consent derivation rules: which permissions derive from which consent types

relationship_permissions.business_reason: Permissions derived from relational proximity between entities. Direct relationships (ownership) grant full access; assigned, participant, org member, and consent-derived relationships grant progressively narrower access.
relationship_permissions.insufficient_current_model: V2 has implicit relationship-based access (e.g., property owner can edit) but no formal relationship graph or distance model.
relationship_permissions.proposed_target: Implement relationship distance model: Direct (owner/creator) → RCEA, Assigned (agent/assignee) → RCE, Participant (party to workflow) → R ± E, OrgMember → per agency_role, ConsentDerived → R (conditional), Public → R (public data only). Build relationship graph resolution function for permission checking.
relationship_permissions.impact: Contextual access control; reduced manual permission assignment; intuitive permission model
relationship_permissions.decision_question: Relationship distance thresholds: which relationships map to which permission strength

direct.examples: Property owner, project creator, document uploader

assigned.examples: Managing agent, assigned lead agent, visit agent

consent_derived.examples: Match-based profile read, purchased lead contact

public.examples: Published property listing, public user profile

transaction_permissions.concept: Transaction permission scoping by party role
transaction_permissions.business_reason: Transaction resources have distinct party roles (demandeur/buyer, holder/seller, agent, notaire) with role-specific permissions within the transaction context.
transaction_permissions.insufficient_current_model: V2 has no Transaction entity. Permissions for transaction-like actions are handled by Project permissions.
transaction_permissions.proposed_target: Implement transaction-specific permission scoping: demandeur (RCE on own transaction data), holder (RCE on own transaction data), agent (RCEA management), notaire (R on assigned transactions), admin (RCEA all). Transaction document permissions follow same party-role model.
transaction_permissions.impact: Transaction data confidentiality; party role enforcement; document access control
transaction_permissions.decision_question: Transaction party permissions: which roles can access which transaction data

system_permissions.concept: System configuration and CRM settings permissions
system_permissions.business_reason: System-level resources (system config, CRM settings) require the most restrictive permissions. Only highest roles can modify system behavior.
system_permissions.source: LAWIM_UNIFIED_DOMAIN_MODEL.md §14.3 (System config and CRM settings rows)
system_permissions.insufficient_current_model: V2 has no formal system config or CRM settings entities. Configuration is done via environment variables or direct DB.
system_permissions.proposed_target: Implement restricted permission model for System config (admin only, Approve level) and CRM settings (admin: RCEA, manager: RA read/approve). System config includes: matching weights, SLA thresholds, scoring parameters. CRM settings includes: routing rules, classification thresholds, anti-fraud configuration.
system_permissions.impact: System integrity; configuration governance; change control

scopes.own: Resources owned by or assigned directly to the user

phase_1_security_foundation.name: Security Foundation
phase_1_security_foundation.focus: Permission levels, matrix, approval workflow, consent model, audit logging

phase_2_scope_and_hierarchy.name: Scope & Hierarchy
phase_2_scope_and_hierarchy.focus: Permission scope, org hierarchy, role intersection, relationship permissions

phase_3_advanced.name: Advanced Features
phase_3_advanced.focus: Temporary grants, consent derivation, dynamic permission resolution
